node.text:
Did we make the grade? Ahead of our #MidYearOutlook release, we discuss in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways what we got right and wrong in our last edition. As anticipated, inflation continued to cool, the Federal Reserve remains on track for a rate cut, and stocks entered a sweet spot. With volatility, recession risk and rate cuts in mind, we did think fixed income would be more competitive with stocks—though we still think bonds have a role to play. 

Tap the link in bio to read on for the full assessment, and to hear what opportunities excite us most.

As we’ve seen this week, the dynamics surrounding inflation, central bank policy, U.S. elections and geopolitics can create volatility. Some are questioning whether it’s time to “get out” of the market or hold on to excess cash—but we think now may actually be the time to get (or stay) invested. Read on for five principles to help you embrace the unknown in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

After three straight months of feeling “down bad,” consumer confidence bounced back in May.

Better sentiment around the job market, business conditions and the stock market boosted spirits, outweighing ongoing worries about higher costs and rates. 

In all, it’s a hopeful sign that sentiment is starting to show what the real data has been saying for months—that consumers are on solid footing, despite the challenges. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
Did you know that wealthy families around the world are increasingly allocating capital to alternative investments? Jamie Lavin Buzzard, Head of U.S. Family Office Investments and Advice, reveals that the 190 wealthy families we surveyed for our 2024 Global Office Family Report allocate 45% of their capital on average to alternatives, which span everything from private equity and private credit to hedge funds. 

To learn more about this and other key insights on family offices, read the full report in the link in bio.

It’s been a story of strength for markets so far this year, fueled by an economy that has remained robust in the face of a host of challenges. As we welcome the start to summer, #TopMarketTakeaways searches for the market’s secret to success and contemplates what could come next. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

In celebration of Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) Heritage Month, we recognize and celebrate the diverse cultures, stories and contributions of our #AAPI colleagues across the firm. #AAPIHeritageMonth

Now in its 25th year, the annual Summer Reading List features a collection of books to reflect upon, inspire and propel us forward. With new titles by preeminent educators, creative entrepreneurs and globally recognized artists and activists, the list takes readers on an adventure from the Peruvian jungle to a Tennessee distillery, Formula 1 racetracks and more.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Listen as Elisa Shevlin Rizzo, Head of Family Office Advisory, introduces our 2024 Global Family Office Report containing detailed feedback from 190 wealthy families around the world. Over 70% of the families surveyed indicate succession planning and educating the rising generation as key objectives but take a variety of approaches in working toward these aims. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

A strong economy is emerging alongside lingering risks, while stock markets power higher. How do investors make sense of it all? This week, #TopMarketTakeaways takes a temperature check on the outlook—what’s too hot, too cold, and just right. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

U.S. inflation cooled for the first time this year. That comes after a hot Q1 sowed some seeds of disinflation doubt.

The details: The April Consumer Prince Index (CPI) showed U.S. headline prices rose 3.4% over the last year, decelerating from 3.5% in March. Similarly, the core measure, stripping out volatile food and energy components, ticked lower to 3.6% from 3.8%. 

Especially notable: More than 55% of the items in the CPI basket are now running at a rate below the Fed’s 2% target. A year ago, more than half were clipping at an above 4% pace. 

Our read: In all, while the journey back to target isn’t over just yet, we are on the way there. That could set policymakers up to deliver their first rate cut toward the end of this year. #jpmorganprivatebank

Corporate America is signaling good things ahead, defying recent challenges. Recession fears have faded, firms are refocusing on investing for future growth and innovation, and shareholders are reaping more rewards. With earnings season winding down, we share what we’ve picked up this week in #TopMarketTakeaways. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Miami set the stage last week for some of the world’s fastest drivers to push the boundaries of speed. Special thanks to #MiamiGP for an exhilarating weekend filled with world-class F1 racing, exclusive events and Miami’s iconic flair. Much like the skill and precision displayed by F1 drivers on track, our commitment to excellence and innovation inspires us to push the limits and strive for greatness in everything we do.

Big tech is putting big money behind AI’s game-changing potential.

The likes of Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft are expected to ramp up their capital expenditures to more than $200 billion over the next 12 months. The lion’s share of this will go toward AI. What’s more, that figure is more than double what the U.S. government is projected to spend this year on education, training, employment and social services. 

Why it matters: These companies are already producing real revenue from their AI investments. As that spending increases, it could unlock even more value—both for big tech and the broader economy. #jpmorganprivatebank

We’re thrilled to announce that J.P. Morgan Private Bank has been named the “Best Digital Private Bank,” “Best Private Bank for Big Data Analytics and AI,” and “Best Private Bank for Digital Customer Experience” by the Professional Wealth Management (PWM) 2024 Global Private Banking Awards. Our team celebrates this honor as we continue to leverage technology to transform our business, drive growth, support our advisors and enhance the client experience. #jpmorganprivatebank

Awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results. Click the link in bio for third-party award selection criteria and other important information.

Despite recent challenges, both the economy and markets have shown resiliency—and we don’t think that signifies a disconnect from reality. We do, however, think that any good investment outlook should take an honest look at the risks. From high rates and frothy valuations to election uncertainty, we share what’s keeping us up at night, and how investors can stay vigilant in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The Federal Reserve signaled it’s staying on hold—and for as long as needed to beat inflation, which is well off its highs, but has recently seen a “lack of further progress.” 

The “why” is important. Growth is good, and it’s just taking longer to get inflation back to its 2% target. Absent unexpected overheating in the economy, the next move still looks like a cut...even if it means it occurs later and the process happens more slowly. That’s still a constructive backdrop for corporate earnings and, by extension, equity markets. 

We’ve seen periods like this before. Back in the 1990s, the Fed held policy rates above 5% for some time, making little tweaks along the way. In the meantime, the economy continued to evade recession for the better part of a decade, the unemployment rate steadily declined, and stocks continued to rally for years. #jpmorganprivatebank

It’s full-throttle excitement as we prepare for the #MiamiGP race weekend. We’re proud to be back as a founding partner, gearing up for high-speed action and unforgettable moments on and off the track. Let the engines roar and the races begin.

node.text:
J.P. Morgan has worked with family offices for more than 200 years, building trusted relationships with many of the world’s wealthiest families. We recently surveyed 190 single-family offices around the globe to gain a better understanding of the challenges and opportunities facing family offices today. We are pleased to share our 2024 Global Family Office Report and invite you to explore some of the key takeaways below. 

Tap the link in bio to read the full report.

The last week has brought both highs and lows. The whiplash, triggered by questions surrounding inflation, big tech and more, prompted a healthy look at our outlook. Digging a layer deeper offers comfort, and stocks have shown resiliency in the face of the year’s challenges. In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we discuss why we still see a constructive path forward. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Earnings season is here, and investors are keen to see if the stock rally has legs.

A good sign is that CEOs are feeling more confident: According to The Conference Board’s gauge, they’re more optimistic about the future than they’ve been in two years.

When CEOs, broader businesses and consumers feel better about the backdrop, it bodes well for increased spending and investment, which fuels growth.

We’re already seeing this take place: Alongside CEOs’ boost to optimism, corporates have been using their extra cash to raise dividends, engage in share buybacks and pursue strategic investments such as AI. 

While there are risks, from geopolitics to “higher for longer rates,” the economy seems to be forging ahead. #jpmorganprivatebank

Is now a good time to buy a luxury home? The short answer is yes. While prices have outpaced the broader market, there are good reasons behind the surge—and there aren’t any signs those factors will change anytime soon. Learn more about the trends and how you might craft a strategic plan for a purchase. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

We recently hosted an important conversation on the impact of social media on our youth with Jonathan Haidt, Ph.D., author of “The Anxious Generation: How the Great Rewiring of Childhood Is Causing an Epidemic of Mental Illness.” Along with Nelle Miller, J.P. Morgan Private Bank Co-Head of New York, he discussed the challenges facing young people today and how we can collectively tackle this problem. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
The J.P. Morgan Private Bank journey with @millemigliaofficial starts here.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Another hot inflation print rattled the markets this week, with traders slashing their rate cut bets. But, this isn't 2022. In the latest #TopMarketTakeaways, we discuss why we think this year can still shape up to be a good one for investors—whether or not the Federal Reserve decides to keep rates where they are.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

With gas prices on the rise as of late, you may be feeling a bit of a pinch at the pump. The spike has come alongside a shortage in oil supply versus strong demand—as OPEC+ recently vowed to continue production cuts until at least June, and the geopolitical tensions in Ukraine and the Middle East continue.

Some worry such high prices might boost inflation, as well as add another pressure point for consumers.

Here’s the good news: While we could see further spikes, we think oil prices (and by extension, fuel prices) will moderate into the end of the year, as higher prices naturally do work to rebalance the market. More supply could come online from the likes of Saudi Arabia and the United States, and consumer demand could lessen. 

So while this transition period may add some pressure to inflation and households, we think the levers are in place to bring some relief as we move through the year. #jpmorganprivatebank

This week’s #TopMarketTakeaways zooms out to examine the larger trends driving markets this year: Inflation is falling, the supply side of the economy is improving, and global growth is accelerating. Read on for why we think investors should embrace the rally. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

There was a lot to like about the first quarter of 2024. Global equities validated 2023’s end-of-year rally with gains in January, February and March, returning +9% for the quarter as a whole. All-time highs were reached by various stock markets around the world, with more companies starting to contribute to the upside. U.S. growth expectations pushed higher, Europe’s economy showed more convincing evidence of stabilization and nascent signs of recovery, and Japan ended the world’s negative interest rate experiment with its first hike in 17 years. 

As the good vibes start to turn some bears into believers, we remind investors that staying invested and sticking to your long-term plan is key in the good and not-so-good times. #jpmorganprivatebank

Markets have continued to rally through Q1, but that doesn’t mean you’ve missed the move. #TopMarketTakeaways examines what’s behind the impressive move higher for stocks and why 
we could see more gains. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Equity securities are subject to "stock market risk" meaning that stock prices in general may decline over short or extended periods of time.
As we continue to highlight #WomensHistoryMonth, our colleagues from around the world shared how they are inspired.

The S&P 500 has made a handful of new all-time-highs so far this year thanks to better-than-expected growth, moderating inflation and Federal Reserve easing—an ideal recipe for corporate profits, and in turn, broader equity returns. 

And it’s not just big tech leading the charge: As stocks march higher, there are signs the rally is broadening out. Compared to last year, 2024 has delivered a much broader trend: Year-to-date, over one-third of S&P 500 companies are actually beating the broader index’s 10% total return, and almost three-quarters are higher overall on the year.

So while investors may be wondering if they have “missed it,” with the potential for more gains ahead, we think the bigger risk is sitting on the sidelines. #jpmorganprivatebank

Rate cuts are officially here and more are on the way. That was the resounding message from central banks this week. As markets take notice, is your portfolio ready? Holding too much cash could be more costly than ever. In the latest #TopMarketTakeaways, we discuss investment considerations to make the most of this shifting environment.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Euromoney has just released its annual Private Banking Awards, and we are proud to announce that J.P. Morgan Private Bank has been named the “World’s Best Private Bank” for the third year in a row, as well as the global leader in the “Next-Gen,” “Philanthropic Advisory” and “Digital Solutions” categories.

We are thrilled to receive these awards, among numerous others, and see them as a testament to how we’re raising the standard in private banking, and delivering exceptional value and service to our clients.

Click the link in bio to learn more about Euromoney’s Private Banking Awards.

Awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results. For third-party award selection criteria and other important information, visit the link in bio. #jpmorganprivatebank

The Federal Reserve stuck to plan but signposted a rosier economy in its latest meeting.

While policymakers held rates steady in March (as expected), they bumped up both their growth and inflation projections. With that in hand, officials maintained their view for three rate cuts this year, and signaled more gradual easing in the years ahead. 

We take this as a sign the Fed has confidence it can get its job done: return inflation to 2% without tipping the economy into a downturn (even if it’s a bumpy ride along the way). In all, the economy seems healthy, not hot. #jpmorganprivatebank

We were a proud sponsor of the recent International Women’s Day Annual Summit hosted by Visionary Women at the Beverly Wilshire Hotel. Visionary Women is a social impact organization of around 300 female founders and executives aimed at promoting the empowerment of women. At this year’s event, attendees heard from speakers including Nicole Avant and Gwyneth Paltrow, and Sharon Stone received the organization’s Changemaker Award presented by our own Malak Santini.
 
Photos by Araya Doheny/Getty Images for Visionary Women

Wishing our friends around the world celebrating Nowruz a very happy Persian New Year. Two of our employees recently shared the significance of this springtime celebration, and what it means to them and their families.

One year after a series of bank failures, where do things stand? Recession fears have faded, and stocks are making new highs, but the landscape isn’t completely unscathed. In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we discuss the progress that’s been made and the risks that remain—and the solutions and opportunities that might lie ahead.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: In the charming city of Maastricht, we are partnering with The European Fine Art Foundation, also known as TEFAF and one of the most prestigious art events in Europe. Walk with us through the Fair as we admire 7,000 years of fine art on display.

February brought another hotter-than-expected inflation print. Some worry progress is stalling. We think it’s more of a speed bump than a roadblock.  Both the headline and core measures of CPI (ex-energy and food) rose +0.4% month-over-month—just a hair over consensus. The spike was mostly from a bump in gasoline prices, which tend to be volatile. Shelter and other core services prices also cooled only slightly.

Despite the heartburn, we don’t think the disinflation trend has been disrupted. On an annual basis, the core measure was its coolest since 2021. And from here, we see powerful forces quelling the pressure—around labor market rebalancing, rent price declines and supply chain normalization.

This all underscores a valuable lesson: The path to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target probably won’t be a linear one. Coupled with a strong economy, this signals rate cuts are probably less urgent than many originally thought…but they’re still coming. We see the first cut happening around mid-year.

At any rate, stocks have continued to march forward despite all the debate. #jpmorganprivatebank
In honor of #WomensHistoryMonth, our colleagues from around the world shared how they are inspired by the month.

The markets keep rolling and the milestones keep coming. From Super Tuesday to Chair Powell’s testimony, we take stock of the latest news cycle and give our own “State of the Union” in this week’s #TopMarketTakeways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

This International Women’s Day, we are celebrating all of the women across our organization making a difference around the world. We asked a few of our leaders at the Private Bank to share inspiring advice for women aiming to achieve new career milestones.

Considered the pinnacle sporting event of the Middle East, the Dubai Duty Free Tennis Championships has a rich history of welcoming some of the top players in the game.

At JPMorgan Chase & Co., we proudly celebrate the 10th anniversary of our partnership in Dubai and enjoy the tournament with our clients from the region. #jpmorganprivatebank

With Super Tuesday now behind us, the road to the 2024 U.S. presidential election is in full swing. A Trump versus Biden stand-off appears to be all but confirmed. 

With that, investor debate on the potential market impact of either candidate is heating up. Policy proposals may well create some buzz, but we are also reminded that stock returns in election and non-election years usually aren’t all that different.

While election years do tend to be more volatile, on average, the S&P 500 has still returned 7.5% in the election years going back to 1928. That compares to non-election years, with an average return of 8%.

Why? Markets just don’t like uncertainty. When that dissipates after results are announced, stocks ultimately tend to rally on a sense of clarity about the path forward. To us, this suggests that keeping long-term goals in mind remains key for investors. #jpmorganprivatebank

What is the predominant vision for energy’s future? Electrify everything with power from solar, wind, transmission and distributed energy storage. While the grid is getting greener, electrification is still slowly advancing. In his 14th annual energy paper, Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy Michael Cembalest takes a closer look at why, and discusses nuclear power, China, hydrogen, “net zero oil” and Gaza’s energy future. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
We’re delighted to announce that we’re embarking on an exciting new chapter in Scotland with the expansion of our team in Edinburgh. 
 
With a deep-rooted connection that spans over a century, we’re thrilled to take the next step in this journey to be closer not only to our clients, but also to the community we serve. 
 
Hear firsthand from Oliver Gregson (Region Head, UK, Channel Islands & Ireland) and colleagues on what this milestone means for us by clicking the link in bio.

As stocks continue to push higher, #TopMarketTakeaways examines some of the key concerns facing investors. Is this market for real? Here are the bull and bear cases—and where we stand. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Various markets around the world have made new all-time highs. While big-tech has been the star of the show thus far, other pockets of the market stand to play catch-up—and some have already started to. Thanks in part to a handful of tailwinds, we see potential for a broadening rally.

So far this year, the average daily session has seen 9% of S&P 500 stocks make a new 52-week high. The average going back to 1990 is around 5%. In other words, this year’s gains have reached beyond big-tech.

Outside of large caps, small- and mid-cap companies could also be poised for growth. The Russell 2000, which represents small-cap companies, remains more than 15% off its last high.

Across markets, themes such as healthcare innovation and ongoing consumer resilience may support recoveries in some areas going forward—and don’t forget about the AI boom, which has only grown louder.

The bottom line? Investors have not “missed it” despite today’s highs, and we still see opportunity on the road ahead. #jpmorganprivatebank

At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we are continuing to honor the contributions and sacrifices of Black individuals who helped shape our nation. To mark #BlackHistoryMonth in the United States, we asked our colleagues to share their stories and what the month means to them. 

“I started my career at JPMorgan Chase after graduating college. It was my first job, having moved away from the Virgin Islands, where I was born and raised. My colleagues welcomed me on their team and also into their personal lives. They became my family away from home, and I didn’t even realize how important that would be to me. In my role as the Asset & Wealth Management Operations DEI Program Manager, I am grateful to be able to make impactful connections with employees as we continue to celebrate the dimensions of difference each employee brings.”

It’s been a record-breaking week. The S&P 500 notched new highs amid the AI boom. Japan made a triumphant return, regaining its prior record after three long decades. Several stock markets across Europe also broke records. With so much enthusiasm, is there more room to climb? In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we explore what’s behind the optimism and where we see opportunity. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Earnings season is coming to a close, and the S&P 500 looks set to notch a second straight quarter of profit growth. That’s after almost a year of contraction. Under the surface, one of the biggest themes and buzzwords continues to be “Artificial Intelligence,” with mentions of it in companies’ earnings calls skyrocketing.

But is it all hype? We don’t think so. The power of AI is already boosting sales. Take Nvidia, for instance. While the chipmaker has rallied a whopping 375% since the start of 2023, that rally has also been underlined by a big boost to earnings, with future expectations also rising.

Longer term, it’s more than just tech that can benefit – companies across industries are making AI investments. As they continue to integrate AI into the fabric of day-to-day operations, we think real cost savings and sales generation are on the horizon. #jpmorganprivatebank

Our strategists have been spanning the globe sharing our investment views for 2024. From inflation and geopolitics to elections and all-time highs, here are the top questions we’re fielding. #TopMarketTakeaways

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Red hot or going blue? The latest U.S. inflation print came on a bit too strong this week, causing some jitters for investors.

But zooming out still shows a lot of disinflation progress. With Valentine’s Day in mind, we took a look at how the prices of our favorite gifts and goods might compare to last year. The bottom line? The pace of price increases has slowed its roll.

This time last year, U.S. headline inflation was running at a 6.4% annual pace. Fast forward to today, that’s more than halved to 3.1%. Much of the decline has been led by core goods and food prices—a dynamic reflected in the cost of romantic meals, jewelry and the like. 

In fact, the average American’s income has gone up by more than our Valentine’s Day basket over the last year, meaning their wage gains have more than offset inflation. In all, while it may be a bumpy path back to the Federal Reserve’s 2% inflation target, the trend of disinflation is still here, and we think the central bank will eventually spread the love with rate cuts around the middle of this year. #jpmorganprivatebank

Engaging with new people and getting involved in the local community are among the many reasons Bankers Chris Morton, Dylan Givner, Melissa Maurer and Jeremy Sunshine are passionate about serving Private Bank clients in Westchester County. Whether you’re focused on building, preserving or transferring your wealth, our specialists are ready to partner with you to help you achieve your financial goals. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Just seven stocks account for almost 30% of the S&P 500 market cap, but are the “Magnificent 7” really that magnificent? In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we break down what we’ve learned from the megacap reports and discuss where we see opportunity. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Has the rally in the “Magnificent 7” stocks (Meta, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, Nvidia, Tesla, and Google) been warranted? We think so, thanks to a few key factors. First, expectations set a high bar for these S&P 500 megacap companies to clear when it came to earnings this season, and they delivered. Further, in addition to AI optimism that is turning into real revenue growth, superior profit margins and a strong return to profitability after a year of outright earnings contraction, the Magnificent 7 companies also lead the rest of the S&P 500 in free cash flow generation. In other words, they have the flexibility to support continued investment in growth opportunities. These fundamental tailwinds and opportunities for innovation have been meaningful drivers for the Magnificent 7. #jpmorganprivatebank

At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we honor the contributions and sacrifices of Black Americans who helped shape our nation. To mark #BlackHistoryMonth in the United States, we asked our colleagues to share their stories and what the month means to them. 

“Growing up as a young, gay Black man, I did not always feel accepted in spaces because of how individuals responded to my mannerisms and the color of my skin. I found my way to J.P. Morgan Private Bank after meeting a group of people at a conference who shared their stories about how supported, understood and safe they feel at the firm. The Private Bank has welcomed me with open arms to be who I fully am, which enables me to bring my whole self to the table as our Head of Credit Card and Partnership Marketing. This is why I share my history at work, because my history is Black history.”

Big rally jitters. An election year. Rate cuts ahead. Seems like déjà vu for markets. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways examines what we’ve learned in the past to help investors prepare portfolios for the future.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Not so fast.

At this week’s meeting, the Federal Reserve signaled interest rate cuts are coming…just don’t expect one in March. 

Policymakers noted they’ve seen a lot of progress on inflation, all while growth has stayed solid. This is good news, but they want to see more of it before making their pivot real.

There are also still a lot of cuts in the cards for 2024, with markets seeing some six rate cuts this year. We think this might be a touch optimistic, but the direction of travel is clear. We see the first cut happening by the middle of the year. 

And while investors seemed to hone in on more of the bad news than the good after the meeting, we’d note the S&P 500 is still up from the prior December meeting (when the Fed first signposted its shift). #jpmorganprivatebank

The S&P 500 just hit a handful of all-time highs. While bears worry about risks such as geopolitics and elections, bulls wonder how much more valuations can expand and how strong earnings can be. In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we examine five catalysts that could move markets in the year ahead.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The promise of living with purpose took on new life at a recent client reception hosted by Olivier de Givenchy, West Region Head for J.P. Morgan Private Bank in Los Angeles. Following opening remarks from Givenchy and Debra Langford, the Private Bank’s Head of Black Wealth Initiatives, guests were joined by diplomat, philanthropist and filmmaker Nicole Avant for a poignant discussion about her recently published memoir Think You’ll Be Happy: Moving Through Grief with Grace, Grit, and Gratitude. Interviewed by Zoë de Givenchy, Avant shared how her faith, inspiring parents and incredible network of friends gave her the power to move beyond her grief and lean in to love and activism. Avant’s book is featured on J.P. Morgan’s 2024 NextList, the firm’s annual collection of books and experiences curated to inspire for the year ahead.

After a long, two-year hiatus, the S&P 500 hit a new all-time high last week. Despite the good vibes and yet another high notched during Monday’s session, some now wonder if there’s more room to run. 

We think so, and that the power for stocks lies in earnings. While changing sentiment can send stocks swinging in the short term, earnings tend to drive stocks in the long term. When you buy a stock, you’re ultimately paying for access to that company’s future profits.

That’s apt, given earnings season for Q4 2023 is in motion. Many companies spent the last year course correcting, retrenching and cutting costs as they saw profits decline. The bad times now seem over: While the results have been a bit mixed so far, it’s still early days. When all is said and done this year, Street analysts anticipate 10 out of 11 S&P 500 sectors will see earnings growth for all of 2024.

While we still may see volatility on the road ahead, we do not think new records are a reason to delay getting invested. #jpmorganprivatebank

During the 40th annual #Sundance Film Festival, we had the honor and privilege to sit down with Jay Ellis, one of Hollywood’s most in-demand multi-hyphenates, and the talented film and television producer Jelani Johnson. The evening consisted of an engaging conversation moderated by our very own Chelsea Crowder-Luke, Executive Director and Private Banker. 

With 200+ clients and guests in attendance, Jay and Jelani shared an exclusive look into their 2024 Sundance film premieres, insights into the major shifts in the industry, and the importance of having diverse thought and backgrounds both in front of and behind the camera. We thank you both for an unforgettable evening.

The lead-up to Election Day can stir up emotions and apprehensions about the future, but we don’t think investors have much cause for concern. In this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways, we uncover the reality behind common election year myths when it comes to markets.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

U.S. election chatter is in full force.

But for all the talk, it’s the underlying macro trends that tend to matter most for markets.

For instance, in 2020, it was the tides of lockdown and reopening from the COVID-19 pandemic that impacted broad markets most, rather than differing ideologies between now-President Biden and then-President Trump. Or, consider 2008 when Democrat Barack Obama ran against Republican John McCain: The unfolding Global Financial Crisis was the predominant driver.

Different sectors could see more of an impact as we get more clarity on the final nominees and their policy proposals, but for broad markets, stocks have historically rallied in the year following an election (on average going back to 1980). 

So while volatility may pick up with the unknown heading into Election Day, markets tend to forge ahead as uncertainty fades. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
After a rally across asset classes ended 2023 with a bang, some investors may be asking if it’s too late to start investing.
 
We don’t think so.
 
Believe it or not, investing at an all-time high hasn’t historically led to a meaningful difference in future returns. In fact, over the last 50 or so years, if you invested in the S&P 500 at an all-time high, your investment would have been higher a year later over 70% of the time with a median return of 12%.
 
Investing at any time (including at records and non-records) also hasn’t made all that much of a difference—with a median one-year return of 10.5% over the same time period. 
 
All that’s to say, we don’t think investors have “missed it.” Ultimately, investing is about time spent in the market, rather than timing it. #jpmorganprivatebank

While it can be difficult to maintain close relationships with siblings into adulthood, families who sustain their wealth usually share a common vision for the future and strive for transparency. Exploring philanthropic causes, researching genealogies and organizing family retreats are just a few of the ways you can reconnect with siblings before collaborating on bigger projects.

node.text:
After a terrible 2022, markets posted big returns to finish 2023 on a high note. 

Both stocks and bonds powered through uncertainty and fears over the recession that never happened, thanks to a stronger-than-anticipated economy, meaningful disinflation and a triumphant end to rate hikes. With a 17% total return, a global 60/40 stock/bond allocation marked its third best year in the last 20—behind only 2009 and 2019.

Some now worry the best gains are in the rearview mirror. But even with 2023’s strong gains in mind, we still see compelling returns for multi-asset investors ahead.

Take, for instance, the S&P 500 Index, which boasted returns of over 20% last year. Out of the 20 other instances that have occurred since 1950, 17 of them (or 80%) have gone on to finish the next year higher. What’s more, the median return the following year has been +12%. 

Past performance is never a guarantee of future results, but so far, our #Outlook2024 seems to be playing out—potentially even better than we anticipated. #jpmorganprivatebank

Just released: Eye on the Market Outlook 2024.

Falling inflation and potential Fed easing are increasing talk of a soft landing rather than a recession and a bear market—but is that already priced in? Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy Michael Cembalest takes a closer look at the investment landscape for 2024, including 10 surprises investors should look out for in the new year. #jpmorganprivatebank

The opportunities here are endless. Discover what Lauren Kessler, Head of U.S. Private Bank Client Service, Experience & Development, and Carly Pierpoint, Head of Global Private Bank and Solutions Talent Acquisition and Growth Strategy, look for in new hires. Find your fit with J.P. Morgan Private Bank.

Click the link in bio to learn more.

Leaders from J.P. Morgan Private Bank gathered with landowners and land management experts from across Texas at our 2023 Landowner Summit to share practical advice and inspired ideas. “It was really great to be back in Texas with our clients and advisor teams at the Landowner Summit,” said Ben Hesse, Chief Financial Officer and Head of Strategy & Business Development, J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management. The event, held in Boerne, Texas, featured a variety of panel discussions on development, innovation and investment for conservation, water scarcity, ranch management and succession planning for family land.

Tap the link in bio to learn more about the event.

node.text: Happy New Year. A young J. Pierpont Morgan penned these words in the very handwriting seen here in an 1854 letter to his parents while studying abroad in Switzerland. Some 17 years later, Morgan and Anthony Drexel would found Drexel, Morgan & Co. in New York. Today, we remain committed to our founders’ principles and the values embedded in our unique 200-plus-year history. Warmest wishes for a wonderful holiday season. #jpmorganprivatebank

The Fed steals the show at year-end, signaling the end of rate hikes and cuts to come. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways looks back at an eventful year for investors and highlights 40 notable events that shaped markets. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

The final Federal Reserve meeting of 2023 is in the books.

Policymakers held rates steady, and rate hikes now seem to be a thing of the past. Inflation continues to cool, growth is slowing but holding up, and a soft landing for the economy looks more likely.

Now, investors are looking to what’s next—and are already betting on when the first rate cut might arrive. Markets think it could happen by March. And according to its own projections, the Fed expects 75 basis points worth of cuts next year, with more in 2025.

But how do markets fare when the first cut comes? We took a quick scan back through history at Fed cutting cycles since 1965. Spoiler: Stocks tend to do well when the Fed is cutting in a soft landing, with the S&P 500 up a meaningful 15% on average 12 months after the first cut.

This jives with our #Outlook2024 expectation that equities can march to new highs next year. #jpmorganprivatebank

The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures in Los Angeles is devoted to the art, science and artists of moviemaking. As a proud partner of the institution, we recently attended its annual gala held to celebrate the power, global impact and indelible importance of cinema.

While shopping and donating to charities this holiday season, take care not to fall victim to fraud. Seasonal scams are increasing every year—in 2022, the FBI estimates that consumers lost more than $282 million due to fraud. Here are a few best practices to shield yourself from swindlers this winter.

node.text:
Three years ago, nearly 30% of all global government debt traded with a negative yield. It seemed the era of super-low interest rates might never end. But then…it did. Today, investors are living in a whole new world, with negative yielding debt almost entirely a thing of the past. Over half of the developed world’s sovereign debt trades with a yield higher than 4%.

The rise in global bond yields is not just historic—it marks the most important development in markets since the world emerged from the COVID-19 pandemic. It has also reconfigured the investing landscape.

Rates near 5% give investors more choices in crafting their goal-aligned wealth plans than at any time since the global financial crisis. Our 2024 Outlook explores how investors can navigate this new range of possibilities and what may come next.

#Outlook2024 #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text:
Just released: J.P. Morgan Private Bank’s 2024 Outlook.

We think the global rate reset offers a once-in-a-generation opportunity. Bonds are more competitive with stocks, inflation will likely settle (but you should still hedge against it), and equities seem to be on a march to new highs. Investors now have more attractive options to consider than at any time in more than a decade. How can you personalize the possibilities? 

Tap the link in bio to read #Outlook2024. 

#jpmorganprivatebank

We were a bit too pessimistic on economic growth, but spot-on in seeing potential for stronger markets. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways takes inventory of where we hit and missed in our 2023 Outlook. Plus, stay tuned for next week’s release of #Outlook2024. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

A recession hasn’t happened. Rate hikes have stopped. Inflation is still cooling. Markets are stronger. There are plenty of reasons for investors to be thankful. This week, #TopMarketTakeaways counts some market blessings and reasons to be optimistic at year-end.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text:
Introducing our Fund Banking Group, a team of over 100 dedicated professionals with over 25 years of experience in the fund space. 

Tap the link in bio to learn more

Is inflation here to spoil your holiday plans? Doesn't look like it.

The latest read on U.S. inflation -- here, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) -- showed that prices cooled more than expected in October. Compared to last month, headline prices didn't increase at all, and core inflation (stripping out volatile food and energy prices) came in at +0.20%. 

If you're planning for your Thanksgiving dinner, we have good news and not-as-good news. The good news: compared to last year, out-of-town guests are looking at lower prices for car rentals, airfares, and gasoline prices. The not-as-good news: you may have a steeper bill when you pick up the turkey and pies.

That ties in with the Fed's latest signaling. While it's still a ways away from getting back to its 2% mandate, inflation has cooled meaningfully. That gives us confidence that policymakers are probably done hiking, and will hold rates "higher for longer" to make sure the data keeps trending in the right direction.

J.P. Morgan celebrated its more than 150-year connection to Paris this week with a number of flagship programs and events. Also, beneath the iconic Eiffel Tower, our guests were treated to an exclusive visit to the Paris Photo Fair, which we’ve proudly sponsored for 12 consecutive years.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

Over 40% of the global economy will see a national election next year, culminating with the United States in November. Do elections matter for markets? This week, #TopMarketTakeaways unpacks why stocks tend to care more about the macro trends.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

With a little under two months left in 2023, we thought it apt to perform a checkup on the economy and markets. The result? While there are plenty of risks swirling around, we still see a path to a soft landing.

Growth is strong but cooling, job gains are still solid but slowing, and inflation has made a lot of progress. The Federal Reserve looks like it’s done hiking, and its “higher for longer” stance seems to ensure the last mile of progress toward central bankers’ goals. 

When it comes to markets, today’s elevated yields offer opportunities for bond investors, and a return to earnings growth this past quarter could help power stocks in the year ahead. 

In all, we think the macro & market vital signs are trending in a positive direction. For more, click the link in our bio for last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways note.

Thanks to the Hudson Valley’s very own Blue Hill at Stone Barns for a wonderful fall evening with beloved clients. The farm-to-table ambiance provided spectacular food, drinks and conversation. Learn more about how our team partners with clients in the Westchester Region.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

At its latest meeting, the Federal Reserve held rates steady for a second time, leaving its policy rate unchanged at 5.50%.

What’s next? While one more hike can’t be ruled out, it looks like we believe the Fed is done making big moves, given all the progress it’s seen in inflation. With the likelihood of further hikes off the table, the Fed seems keen on keeping policy rates “higher for longer” to ensure it can claim victory over inflation.

This has important implications for investors. When the Fed finishes hiking, yields typically tend to fall as investors gain clarity on the path forward, and as growth and inflation slow. That means cash is often faced with reinvestment risk, and other asset classes tend to offer better opportunities. 

We’ve seen that dynamic play out over the last seven Fed hiking cycles. On average, after the final hike, municipal bonds, stocks and core fixed income have all meaningfully outperformed cash over the following two years. 

So while cash may seem like a safe play now, investors may want to consider rethinking how much they’re holding. 

Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

#TopMarketTakeaways unpacks a jam-packed week, and how it may bring more comfort for investors as we move toward year-end.

Tap the link in bio to learn more.

node.text: Our 2nd Annual Global Digital Innovation Competition is here.  Watch some of our contestants prepare for their presentations today in New York as they try to bring the next big idea to #jpmorganprivatebank.

A crowd of wine enthusiasts gathered to enjoy “The Urban Grape Experience,” a guided wine tasting hosted by J.P. Morgan’s Black Wealth Initiatives team in Boston. The exclusive event, the team’s first in the city, was curated by TJ Douglas, Founder and CEO of The Urban Grape, and featured wines produced by Black vintners from around the world.

This week marked our 11th Annual J.P. Morgan/Robin Hood Investors Conference in New York City, which featured professionals in investing, tech, AI and global policy. The event has raised nearly $60 million since 2013, 100% of that used to benefit the fight against poverty in New York City.
